name: JTBD Four Forces
prompt: Use JTBD Four Forces to stress-test the requirement: push of the current situation, pull of the new solution, anxiety about switching, and habit/inertia holding the old behavior. Anchor every finding in a concrete past event, observed quote, or local evidence; do not ask only hypothetical preference questions.

calibration.business: Use stakeholder-facing wording: what happened, what pushed the current behavior, what pulled them toward change, what worried them, and what existing habit may resist the switch.
calibration.technical: Use delivery-facing wording: observed trigger, target capability pull, switching anxiety, operational inertia, affected interfaces/data/processes, and evidence that the event already happened.

evidence_contract: Every JTBD force finding must cite a verbatim local event, quote, or requirement fragment. Pure hypothetical phrasing must remain a guardrail signal, not a confirmed gap closure.
